name: Tool Steel Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Tool Steel. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Tool Steel oxide removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength with good absorption in Tool Steel oxides and contaminants

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective cleaning resolution and energy density control

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning coverage with controlled thermal input

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without damaging Tool Steel substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient contaminant ablation with minimal thermal diffusion

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for efficient area coverage while maintaining cleaning effectiveness

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal from Tool Steel surfaces

overlapRatio.description: Optimal pulse overlap for uniform cleaning coverage without excessive thermal buildup

power.description: Typical range for industrial 1064 nm laser cleaning of tool steels to remove contaminants without excessive heating; recommended starting value balances efficiency and safety. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs for metal surface preparation (50-500 W common), with max limited to avoid melting (tool steel melting point ~1400-1500°C).

scan_speed.description: Range for effective contaminant removal on tool steel, slower for heavy rust/oil, faster for light dust; 2000 mm/s optimal for uniform cleaning at moderate power. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on steel ablation.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime typical for industrial cleaning to achieve ablation with minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ <10 μm on tool steel); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise but costlier applications. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2020) showing ns pulses optimal for metals with high thermal conductivity like tool steel.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed fiber lasers in cleaning; 50 kHz recommended for tool steel to enable fast processing while limiting heat accumulation (trade-off: higher rates increase speed but risk thermal buildup). Source: Academic literature in Journal of Laser Applications (2019) on pulse train effects in metal cleaning.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation on tool steel (reflectivity ~0.4 at 1064 nm); below damage threshold of ~4-5 J/cm² to prevent surface pitting. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Surface and Coatings Technology (2017) for alloy steels, conservative for tool steel's hardness.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for cleaning tool steel; smaller for fine details (e.g., tool edges), larger for broad surfaces; 200 μm recommended for balanced resolution and coverage. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems for industrial metal processing.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy oxide layers on tool steel; 2 passes typical for complete removal without substrate damage. Source: Industry case studies in Proceedings of SPIE (2021) on laser cleaning of hardened steels.

overlap_ratio.description: Overlap ensures uniform fluence distribution on reflective tool steel surfaces; 50% recommended for even cleaning without gaps or over-ablation. Source: Process optimization studies in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2019), standard for scanned beam paths.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

Tool Steel surface undergoing laser cleaning showing precise contamination removal
Microscopic view of Tool Steel surface after laser cleaning showing detailed surface structure
